sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Wipro Stock: Quiet Rally, Big Question for US Tech Investors
feed.summary: Wipro Ltd's US-listed shares (ADRs) have seen a quiet rally driven by stronger IT services demand and cost discipline, yet they still trade at a discount compared to many US tech peers. For US investors, Wipro acts as a leveraged play on US tech spending with an emerging-markets overlay, offering a way to diversify tech exposure at a potentially lower price point. The company's performance is closely tied to global enterprise tech spending cycles and India's structural growth, making it a satellite tech holding for US-centric portfolios.

feed.title: 3 REITs Every Investor Should Know About
feed.summary: This article highlights three top REITs: Federal Realty Investment Trust, Realty Income, and Prologis, recommending them for long-term investors due to their consistent dividend growth and strong business models. Federal Realty is noted for its Dividend King status and focus on high-quality suburban properties. Realty Income is recognized for its stable monthly dividends from a diversified global portfolio, and Prologis for its rapid dividend growth and strategic investments in logistics and data centers.

feed.title: SOFI Stock Today: February 21 — TransUnion Flags 2026 Personal-Loan Boom
feed.summary: TransUnion's 2026 outlook predicts that unsecured personal loans will be the primary engine of loan growth, with fintech lenders expected to increase their market share, which bodes well for SoFi's core personal-loan sector. However, rising subprime demand and delinquencies signal potential challenges related to credit costs and the need for tighter underwriting. Technically, SOFI stock is oversold with strong downtrend indicators, suggesting volatility and the importance of risk management leading up to its Q1 earnings report on April 28, 2026.

feed.title: Altria Group Inc.: Hidden High-Yield Play Or Value Trap For 2026?
feed.summary: Altria Group Inc. is presented as a high-yield investment due to its massive dividend cash flow, despite facing increasing regulatory pressure, vape bans, and shifting consumer habits in the US nicotine market. The article explores whether Altria is a smart play for passive income or a potential value trap, emphasizing its US market dominance and the ongoing tension between its strong income generation and significant regulatory and social trend risks. For investors, it's highlighted as a high-scrutiny position requiring active monitoring of regulations and consumer shifts.

feed.title: Rackspace shares rocket as it partners with Palantir on artificial intelligence
feed.summary: Rackspace Technology Inc. has partnered with Palantir Technologies Inc. to help its customers deploy and utilize artificial intelligence solutions. This collaboration will allow Rackspace to integrate Palantir's Foundry and Artificial Intelligence Platform into its customer systems, addressing evolving computing needs driven by AI. Following the announcement, Rackspace shares surged by 226%, closing at $1.68, reflecting investor optimism despite recent quarterly losses and leadership changes at the company.

feed.title: Former Worthington Industries Steel Plant Sold
feed.summary: The former Worthington Industries coil processing facility in Cleveland has been sold for an amount exceeding its $15 million asking price. The 33-acre site was acquired by 4600 Premier Holding LLC and 4600 Heidtman Parkway Holding LLC, with Reserve Management Group, an affiliate of one of the buyers, planning ferrous and nonferrous metals recycling services at the location. The plant, which once employed 100 non-union workers, had only 55 employees by the time Worthington shut it down.
